ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user who posted the tweet */,
  "screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "tweet_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the tweet was posted */,
  "original_tweet" TEXT /* Content of the original tweet */,
  "sentiment" TEXT /* Sentiment classification of the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been retweeted */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source platform of the tweet */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language in which the tweet is written */,
  "hashtags" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of hashtags used in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_location FOREIGN KEY ("location") REFERENCES locations ("location_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "user_location" TEXT /* Location specified by the user */,
  "account_creation_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of accounts the user follows */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is verified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment category */,
  "sentiment_name" TEXT /* Name of the sentiment (e.g., Positive, Negative) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sentiment category */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Numerical score representing the sentiment intensity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region or area where the location is situated */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets_sentiments" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sentiment classification associated with the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "sentiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report findings */,
  "detailed_report" TEXT /* Detailed content of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who generated the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., analyst, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_assignments" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the platform */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view report, analyze tweet) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the platform */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser or device used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
